voice_notes: Direct and technical. Short sentences. Lead immediately with what you know about their specific program or signal — no preamble. Reference the signal in the first line. Close with a single simple question: '20 minutes?' or 'Worth a call?'. Never open with 'I hope this finds you well' or 'I wanted to reach out.' Founder-to-engineer energy. Mention real program names, real specs, real timelines. No feature dumping. The ask is a conversation, not a demo.

persona.title: Chief Technology Officer / Mission Architect
persona.why_they_feel_it: Building a GPS-independent positioning constellation from scratch. Every payload decision is a precedent-setting one. Heritage requirements from legacy suppliers add 12-18 months they don't have.

accounts.angle: Your PULSAR constellation needs payload suppliers who can certify on your schedule, not a heritage provider's — Peregrine's active flight program and commercial qualification process is built for exactly this.

why_now_signal.description: Xona raised a $19M Series A to accelerate PULSAR constellation deployment. Now actively hiring mission systems engineers for payload integration.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payloads qualified to Xona's SWaP envelope and schedule. Peregrine absorbs heritage risk — Xona gets flight-ready hardware on commercial terms without a 2-year certification cycle.
accounts.heritage_risk: Low — commercial NewSpace, no government heritage requirement on PULSAR
accounts.notes: Explicitly called out in done condition for outreach generator test. High-priority test case.

persona.title: VP Engineering / Head of Payload Systems
persona.why_they_feel_it: Running a pure-RF constellation and watching customers ask for optical fusion to complete the intelligence picture. Multi-modal is the next capability gap and they know it.

accounts.angle: Adding optical to your RF constellation turns signal detection into confirmed attribution — Peregrine can deliver an optical payload that integrates with your existing bus architecture without heritage-blocking the program.

why_now_signal.description: HawkEye 360 awarded multi-year DOD SIGINT analytics contract, expanding constellation to Cluster 7+. Actively sourcing next-generation payload capabilities.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payload that integrates with HE360's existing satellite bus and ground segment. Certification path designed for commercial constellations, not legacy defense programs.
accounts.heritage_risk: Low — commercial procurement, mission-driven not heritage-driven

persona.title: VP Payload Engineering
persona.why_they_feel_it: Responsible for Gen-3 satellite payload upgrades. Legacy optical suppliers have 18-month lead times and require heritage certifications that don't fit BlackSky's 12-month refresh cycle.

accounts.angle: Your Gen-3 constellation timeline is constrained by payload supplier lead times — Peregrine delivers qualified optical payloads faster and on commercial terms, without blocking your manifest.

why_now_signal.description: BlackSky announced Gen-3 satellite program with upgraded resolution and revisit rates. Payload procurement underway. Public roadmap references new optical capability.

accounts.product_fit: Higher-resolution optical payload that fits BlackSky's existing bus platform, with qualification timeline matching their Gen-3 launch manifest.
accounts.heritage_risk: Low — NYSE-listed commercial company, procurement on commercial terms

persona.title: Mission Architecture Lead / VP Engineering
persona.why_they_feel_it: Optimizing cost-per-image at scale for the next constellation tranche. Every payload dollar matters. Traditional optical suppliers price for custom programs, not commodity-scale production.

accounts.angle: If you're sourcing payloads for your next tranche at scale, a supplier with an active flight program and commodity-level pricing removes the cost ceiling that slows constellation expansion.

why_now_signal.description: Satellogic signed a new government analytics contract and publicly committed to constellation expansion. Next satellite tranche planning is underway.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payloads priced for production-scale constellation builds, with Peregrine absorbing NRE and heritage certification costs.

persona.title: CTO / VP Systems Engineering
persona.why_they_feel_it: Built the best SAR constellation in commercial space and is now being asked by customers for optical fusion. SAR alone is no longer the full answer. Adding optical without building a second constellation is the problem.

accounts.angle: SAR plus optical fusion is becoming the baseline for premium EO — Peregrine can add optical capability to your constellation without requiring you to build a separate program from scratch.

why_now_signal.description: Umbra closed a $35M Series C to expand constellation and add new sensing modalities. CTO has publicly discussed multi-modal sensing as a 2024-2025 priority.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payload that co-manifests with Umbra's SAR satellites on Rocket Lab or SpaceX rideshare. Same ground segment, different sensing modality.

persona.title: VP Business Development / Program Manager
persona.why_they_feel_it: Winning SDA and DoD bus contracts but getting blocked at payload integration because heritage-qualified optical suppliers can't hit SDA's 18-month cadence. The bus is ready. The payload is the constraint.

accounts.angle: SDA Tranche programs need payload partners who can certify on 18-month cycles — Peregrine qualifies to MIL-SPEC timelines and absorbs heritage risk so York's bus business doesn't get held up at payload integration.

why_now_signal.description: York Space awarded SDA Transport Layer contracts for satellite bus production. Active payload integration partner search underway for Tranche 2 delivery.

accounts.product_fit: Payload supplier who integrates with York's S-class bus, meets SDA schedule requirements, and handles qualification certification — removing the integration bottleneck from York's program.
accounts.heritage_risk: Medium — SDA has formal qualification requirements, but Peregrine's flight program addresses this

persona.why_they_feel_it: Running OPIR and SDA programs under DoD schedule pressure. Primary payload suppliers have 24+ month lead times and heritage requirements that add cost and schedule risk to every program.

accounts.angle: Your SDA and OPIR programs are constrained by payload suppliers with 24-month backlogs — Peregrine is building flight heritage now and can be on your approved vendor list before your next RFP drops.

why_now_signal.description: General Atomics EMS awarded SDA Tranche 2 tracking layer contract. Subcontractor solicitation for optical payload components active.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payload components qualifiable to MIL-SPEC with GA-EMS as integrating prime. Peregrine handles heritage certification pathway, GA-EMS handles system integration.
accounts.heritage_risk: Medium-High — DoD program, formal qualification required. Mitigated by Peregrine's active flight program.

persona.title: VP Engineering / Program Manager
persona.why_they_feel_it: Scaling satellite manufacturing for both commercial and government customers. Payload supplier qualification is a recurring bottleneck — approved vendor lists don't grow as fast as build rates.

accounts.angle: As your manufacturing rate increases, the approved payload vendor list becomes the ceiling — Peregrine's qualification path is designed to get onto that list faster than legacy suppliers.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payload with qualification documentation ready for LeoStella's vendor approval process. Built to integrate with their bus platform with standard ICD.

persona.title: VP Mission Systems / Director of Satellite Systems
persona.why_they_feel_it: Selling end-to-end mission solutions and getting asked for payload capabilities Rocket Lab doesn't build in-house. Preferred payload suppliers who can move on Electron/Neutron timelines are a competitive differentiator.

accounts.angle: If you're selling full mission solutions, Peregrine as a preferred optical payload partner gives you a capability that fits your satellite buses and your delivery timelines without adding a heritage bottleneck.

accounts.product_fit: Optical payload sized for Photon buses, qualified on timelines that match Rocket Lab's 6-9 month launch cadence.
accounts.heritage_risk: Low-Medium — depends on end customer. Commercial missions are heritage-agnostic; government missions require qualification.

persona.title: Principal Investigator / Senior Research Scientist
persona.why_they_feel_it: Running instrument development programs where the goal is building new sensing capability, not procuring commodity hardware. Heritage requirements don't exist in R&D — performance and schedule do.

accounts.angle: NRL programs building new optical sensing capabilities need hardware suppliers who can deliver research-grade qualification data on government timelines — Peregrine is currently building flight hardware, not paper studies.

why_now_signal.description: NRL issued multiple SBIR solicitations for advanced optical sensing payloads and smallsat instrument components. Phase II awards active for optical systems.

accounts.product_fit: Research-grade optical payload with full characterization data, available for integration with NRL smallsat programs. Peregrine can provide custom aperture configurations.
accounts.heritage_risk: Low — R&D context, heritage requirements do not apply to instrument development programs
accounts.notes: Research programs often become the path to first flight heritage. High strategic value even if revenue is smaller.

accounts.angle: MITLL instrument programs regularly work with early-heritage hardware suppliers who can move on research timelines — this is the path where Peregrine gets first-flight data and MITLL gets capability they can actually build with.

why_now_signal.description: MIT Lincoln Laboratory is actively developing responsive space sensing systems under DARPA and AFRL programs. Component solicitations for optical subsystems visible in recent procurement activity.

accounts.heritage_risk: Low — university-affiliated research, prototype-grade hardware is the expectation
